mnemonics.title: Array Name Is Constant Pointer
mnemonics.content: ARRAY NAME IS LIKE A READ-ONLY GPS: It always points to the starting location (first element) and you cannot make it point elsewhere. But you CAN use it to navigate to other elements.

mnemonics.title: Pointer Arithmetic Rules
mnemonics.content: ADD SUBTRACT OK, MULTIPLY DIVIDE NO. You can add/subtract integers to pointers but cannot multiply or divide two pointers.

mnemonics.title: Malloc vs Calloc
mnemonics.content: MALLOC = MEMORY ALLOCATION (raw/uninitialized), CALLOC = CLEAN ALLOCATION (zero-initialized). Think C for Clean/Clear.

mnemonics.title: Asterisk Position Matters
mnemonics.content: INT *P = POINTER TO INT; INT *P[5] = ARRAY OF 5 POINTERS TO INT; INT (*P)[5] = POINTER TO ARRAY OF 5 INTS. The asterisk position decides everything!

acronyms.usage: Allocates specified bytes at runtime. malloc(n) allocates n bytes of uninitialized memory.
Allocates memory for n elements of specified size, initialized to zero. calloc(n, size).
Changes size of previously allocated memory block. Can expand or shrink memory.
Represents null pointer constant, defined as ((void*)0). Means pointer points nowhere.
Operator that returns size in bytes. Used with types or variables.
Counts characters in string excluding null terminator. Part of string.h.

visualTips.visualization: DRAW CONTIGUOUS BOXES IN A ROW. Each box is one element. Number them 0,1,2... with indices written below. Show arrow from array name to first box.
DRAW A BOX FOR VARIABLE WITH VALUE. Draw ANOTHER BOX for pointer containing an arrow pointing TO the first box. The arrow represents the address.
SHOW A NUMBER LINE WITH ADDRESSES: 1000, 1004, 1008... for int (4 bytes). Show ptr+1 jumping 4 units, ptr+2 jumping 8 units.
DRAW A GRID OF BOXES. Number them sequentially: [0][0], [0][1], [0][2]... then [1][0], [1][1]... Fill in order, showing rows stacked horizontally.
DRAW HEAP AS A CLOUD. Show malloc allocating a block, drawing arrow from pointer to that block. Show free cutting the connection.

analogies.analogy: ARRAY NAME IS LIKE A HOTEL NAME. The hotel name (array name) always refers to the same hotel (first element address). You can't change the hotel name, but you can ask for room 5 (arr[5]).
POINTER IS LIKE A STREET ADDRESS. Adding 1 to a pointer is like moving to the next house number - you don't jump 1 byte, you jump the size of one 'house' (element).
MALLOC IS LIKE GETTING A NEW APARTMENT - empty, you furnish it yourself. CALLOC IS LIKE GETTING A FURNISHED APARTMENT - ready with zeroed furniture.
DANGLING POINTER IS LIKE A PHONE NUMBER TO A DEMOLISHED BUILDING. The number exists but points to nothing useful anymore. Calling it gives unpredictable results.
POINTER TO POINTER IS LIKE A MAILING LIST THAT HAS ANOTHER LIST'S ADDRESS. To find the actual data, you follow two arrows instead of one.
